title: Tell me about yourself — walk me through your background and resume.

transcript.text: Tell me about yourself — walk me through your background and resume.

blocks.md:
## "Tell me about yourself / walk me through your resume" — assuming a strong-tech-company hiring-manager round (no company named, so I'm keeping it framework-neutral; if it's Amazon/Google/DO, say the word and I'll tilt it)

This one is NOT STAR. It's a tight career arc: who I am now → the few projects that matter → why I'm here. ~150 words, ~60 seconds.

🎙️ **Say-it script:**

> 💬 "So I'm a backend engineer, about three and a half years in, mostly high-throughput distributed systems. Right now I'm a Software Engineer III at Walmart, on the Data Ventures team — we build the supplier-facing data APIs that companies like Pepsi and Coca-Cola use. My main thing there was an audit-logging library that does about 2 million events a day. Twelve-plus teams pull it in with one dependency, and it replaced a vendor tool that cost us around fifty grand a month with a custom pipeline closer to five hundred.
>
> Before Walmart I was at a startup, Good Creator Co. Small team — I was basically the sole architect for six production services in Go and Python. The one I'm proudest of is a ClickHouse migration that took our analytics queries from 30 seconds down to 12 and cut infra cost about 30%.
>
> And I started at PayU in loan disbursal — first real on-call, real customer money. I got a partner API failure rate from 4.6% down to under a third of a percent.
>
> What I'm after next is platform work at bigger scale and a higher ownership level. That's why I'm here."

💬 **Even-shorter version (~20s):**

> 💬 "Backend engineer, three and a half years. Right now SE-III at Walmart Data Ventures on supplier data APIs — built an audit pipeline doing 2 million events a day. Before that, sole architect of six services at a startup, including a ClickHouse migration that cut query time and cost. Started in fintech at PayU. I'm looking for platform work at bigger scale."

---

**Tips for delivery:**
- Keep it to three stops: Walmart (now) → Good Creator Co. (range/ownership) → PayU (where it started). Don't list every project — name one number per stop and move on.
- Land on the "why I'm here" line and then stop. Let them pick what to dig into.
- Have your follow-up depth ready: if they say *"tell me more about the audit library"* → go to W2; *"the ClickHouse one"* → G1; *"sole architect of six?"* → G7.

**What NOT to say:**
- Don't bad-mouth Walmart or say you're "bored" — frame leaving as wanting bigger scale and ownership, full stop.
- Don't recite the whole stack list — it sounds like a résumé read-out. Numbers and impact only.
- Don't say "GCC" — say "Good Creator Co." once, then "the startup."

Want me to tailor this to a specific company (Amazon LP-flavored, Google, DigitalOcean's 7 values), or run the likely first follow-up?
